recall_context.query: Who is expected to own the rollback evidence pack if the July 26 milestone slips?

extraction_items.text: Agreed to use full end-to-end dry run as deployment validation path. Decision made on 2025-07-10.
@ProjectManager, please freeze the validation path in the spec today: http://sharepoint/treasury_management_system_implementation/production_deployment_readiness_spec
Confirmation required by EOD Friday (2025-07-11) from Operations, Support, and Integration roles to avoid being flagged as readiness risk.
Deployment window target is July 19, 2025. To be kept protected per product owner.
Owner for exception sign-off: @Ops; Updater for threshold changes: @QA.
FIFO and severity overrides to be retained unless 10-business-day trigger conflicts with July 27 reporting cutoff.
Need Ops to confirm that the 10-business-day trigger does not conflict with July 27 reporting, QA, or handoff SLAs before locking the spec.
Decision to use full dry run for production deployment readiness.
Spec must be frozen today, 2025-07-10.
Primary/backup coverage and access approvals must be signed off by EOD Friday, 2025-07-11.
Proposed: freeze current contract and have Finance sign off on ledger-structure delta before test handoff
Hard gate with Change Manager sign-off by 3 p.m. local time.
Ops Lead to publish trigger criteria and freeze time by EOD 2025-07-20.
Change Manager to sign only after Ops Lead publishes trigger criteria and freeze time by EOD 2025-07-20.
Any missed 3 p.m. signoff treated as automatic exception path.
Backlog surge is tracked to User_5's target date of 2025-07-27.
Compliance Officer decided to keep FIFO as the control for case backlog, with only hard-coded severity exceptions and a documented escalation rule.
Proposed ownership for July 28 cutoff: QA to confirm impact, Compliance to own fallback if evidence logging slips or cutoff shifts. Awaiting lock-in.
Phase is live at 0% complete for July 28 window
Reference to cutover downtime specification document.
lock primary/backup contacts and evidence owner in the runbook; confirm who owns go/no-go rollback call if handoff slips
Cutover scoped to controls, rollback, and support coverage; lower-priority readiness work paused to avoid instability into July 28.
Rollback stays the decision point; access approval follows evidence.
@Change Manager: confirm 3 p.m. local signoff and same-day CAB logging for exceptions.
@Ops Lead: send blocker list plus exact freeze time by EOD.
If validation evidence slips past July 26, Support stays on reduced coverage, Change logs exception, Ops owns restored-proof decision before signing rollback.
@Ops Lead confirm exact slip trigger; @Change Manager confirm who logs exception after hours.
Finance and Support have a real go/no-go control for July 28 based on rollback decision and access approval.
Cutover owner assigned to Ops Lead
Rollback approval assigned to Change Manager
War-room coverage requires a 24x7 handoff map
@Ops Lead and @Support Lead to confirm owners, coverage hours, and approval path against http://sharepoint/treasury_management_system_implementation/risk:_cutover_downtime_spec by EOD
If July 26 misses, @Change Manager logs the miss, @Support Lead drops to reduced-war-room, @Ops Lead owns frozen artifact and rollback evidence pack
14% complete; extraction logic in place; validating source mappings; adjusting integration layer due to late change in source ledger structure.
Finance Data Owners need to sign off on current field mapping; without sign-off, report generation and reconciliation are at risk.
Freeze current contract conditional on Finance signing ledger delta and rollback pack being complete.

Requires confirmation of backup contacts and rollback path from @ProjectManager before committing to full dry run; past support staffing gap and weak fallback ownership in Financial Reporting Automation are concerns.

extraction_items.text: Lock rerun timestamp, exact build hash, and intended rerun slot into the gate today

Banking owns reversal evidence
QA hard-rejects anything incomplete
UAT readiness target set to July 27
Blocked on ACK timing validation; ownership: Banking; mapping to spec by EOD July 21
Blocked on reconciliation output; ownership: ERP
Blocked on exception routing evidence; ownership: Treasury Ops
Interface outputs mismatching integration spec; causing repeated defects and slowing reconciliation testing
Require Banking's reversal thread, Treasury confirmation, and matching rerun details in spec before QA seals; Finance Ops/Reporting may not see bundle before Friday
Not locking new field definition; keep impacted scenarios in triage lane until failure pattern proven, then update spec
Map top three blockers against spec by EOD July 21
Primary spec and evidence bundle for integration testing revision
Freeze mappings for this revision window; treat any drift as formal change.
Blocker-first approach agreed; owner/readiness list must call out open dependencies; lock owner/readiness by EOD July 20 as gate for July 27 retests.

One coordinator; joint rerun gate: no control-owner field, no signoff, no retest until Banking, ERP, Treasury Ops match spec; blocker list limited to ACK timing, approval rejection, exception routing, audit bundle gaps; lock by EOD July 23.
Banking to post bundle in integration testing spec by EOD Friday.
QA can seal once banking bundle lands; payment validation and bank response timing remain as only open retests.
Only flag ERP and banking issues as blockers when they block ACK timing, reconciliation, or exception routing; escalate against spec.
User_1 to publish defect owners and test data readiness by EOD July 20.

Control-break defects have 24-hour retest SLA; evidence capture for interface, ops, and reporting sign-off against http://sharepoint/treasury_management_system_implementation/integration_testing_cycle___revision_spec; banking slip triggers immediate escalation.
QA reject incomplete bundles by EOD.

Make hard QA reject for rerun slot not matching window; Banking post owner/ETA in same thread by EOD; @User_3 update spec today.
QA stays sealed until evidence chain complete with defect ID, rerun timestamp, build hash, and env ID; Treasury and Reporting confirmations today, timestamped in-thread.
@User_3 lock bundle hard-block conditions into spec gate today.
Banking post full bundle in http://sharepoint/treasury_management_system_implementation/integration_testing_cycle___revision_spec by EOD Friday 2025-08-01.

Finance assigned payment defects, Operations assigned reporting validation; both must confirm impacted interfaces and timing against http://sharepoint/treasury_management_system_implementation/integration_testing_cycle___revision_spec by 2025-07-25.
Banking must post full bundle (owner, ETA, evidence IDs) in http://sharepoint/treasury_management_system_implementation/integration_testing_cycle___revision_spec by EOD 2025-07-27.
QA seals only after timestamp, build hash, evidence ID, ETA, and cut-off window are all in-thread.
After bundle fields are posted, lock blocker list to payment validation and bank response timing only.

extraction_items.text: Phase at 95%. Remaining gate: wording sign-off. Deadline to close: before July 29.
Framework kept flexible with explicit decision points.

Legal must confirm wording and define wording-change threshold; Risk must define decision tree for late drift; Compliance must log decision, owner/trigger/evidence path, and control-to-requirement mapping in spec. Control ownership and reporting timing also blockers.
Baseline frozen on current version; changes routed through controlled change.
Phase is 99% complete, pending final sign-off from Legal, Risk, and Sustainability on disclosure assumptions, owner, and evidence-path wording.
Legal and Risk must confirm clause, owner, trigger, disclosure timing, and evidence path in the spec before phase can be marked Completed. Deadline: end of day 2025-07-27.
Legal and Risk must confirm clause, owner, trigger, evidence path by end of day 2025-07-27, then Compliance logs and phase set to Completed.
Completion held until Legal, Risk, Compliance explicitly capture owner, trigger, evidence-path in spec.
Legal to own final clause text tonight; Risk to confirm evidence-path impact; Compliance to log decision in spec.
Phase completion blocked by Legal and Risk confirmations on threshold wording, evidence standards, and control-ownership line in the spec.

recall_context.query: What are the three key controls identified in FRA for stabilizing reconciliation?

extraction_items.text: 43% through the risk phase.
Finance, Ops, IT must confirm ownership changes by Friday to avoid fast interim fix.
Fast interim fix for July 18.
Primary, backup, and approval authority defined for reconciliation, incident response, and monitoring.
ProjectManager to stamp primary/backup/approver for reconciliation, incident response, and monitoring by today.
Support to validate the live roster against the frozen chain before rehearsal closes.
Risk formally in scope. Treasury, Integration, Support to review spec and return initial findings by EOD.
Treasury to post fallback thresholds language in the spec today.
Integration to add three fully replayed cases with timestamps, message IDs, and payload hashes by EOD.
Treasury and Integration to confirm hard-stop criteria before Friday.
Treasury, Integration, Support to provide initial findings by EOD.
Controls missing owner, design, or operating evidence are treated as open until proven otherwise. Legal or Ops must tag downstream reporting impact in matrix immediately to change remediation order.
Provisional items can only close when Ops names source, versioned artifact link, and timestamp. 'Reporting ready' is internal-only. Each provisional control should have a single owner.
Gap assessment, owner locking, and confirmation of procedural vs real exposure must be completed before 2025-07-18 cutoff.
Legal and Ops input is gating on a few controls. Ownership confirmation needed against spec.
Phase is 99% complete. Remaining tasks: tighten gap assessment, lock remediation owners, confirm procedural vs real exposure.
Integration Testing is underway at 3% completion.
Prefer narrow end-to-end pass covering payment files, bank connectivity, reconciliation, reporting, and exception paths at least once against the spec.
Treasury to post trigger text including exact hard-stops in the spec today.
Integration to replay 3 failures with timestamps, message IDs, and payload hashes, and tag each replay to config, mapping, or downstream behavior.

Extraction pipelines are 21% in; audit trail is key watchout; awaiting confirmation of reconciliation rules and sign-off evidence from Finance and Data Engineering per spec at http://sharepoint/financial_reporting_automation/build_data_extraction_pipelines_spec
User_12 covers control validation for posting, reconciliation, and exception logging traceability.
Integration to confirm message-handling defects for interface triage.
Treasury and Support to lock fallback path by today (2025-07-19).

Freeze separate primary, backup, and approver for reconciliation, incident response, and monitoring. Capture one gap, one dependency, one rollback trigger per group in runbook. @ProjectManager to stamp owner chain, @Support to validate live roster before rehearsal closes.
Runbook for Production Deployment Readiness
Keep fallback sign-off on Treasury for EOD Friday.
Lost a day because interface triage and controls review were split too late.
Wait for consolidated defect list in SharePoint before closing out.
Keep defect list and sign-off path separate until owners are clear. Decision made to prevent rushing controls in Northstar Reporting. Friday sign-off agreed.
Treasury asked to post fallback criteria for Payment Interface Failures.
Integration asked to tag which failures are config vs mapping.
Unowned control gaps may slip past 2025-07-18 cutoff and delay downstream reporting
Phase kicked off at 6% complete. Mapping gaps to ownership, confirming compensating controls, flagging impacts for Legal, Ops, Internal Audit.
Referenced as the evidence source for confirming owner, system touchpoints, and evidence source.
Dependency confirmation needed for file transfers, bank interface validation, and exception handling. Hard sign-off required.
Finance is fallback owner. Payment-format changes touching mapping, reconciliation, or bank-response fields require full re-validation; delta only for presentation-only changes.
Confirmation of fallback owner and re-test trigger due by EOD 2025-07-09.
Integration testing spec referenced for the cycle.

Payment interface risk formally identified and in scope at 8% complete. Review spec at http://sharepoint/treasury_management_system_implementation/risk:_payment_interface_failures_spec and flag interface gaps, exception paths, or reconciliation concerns this week to protect July 26 rollout.

candidates.subject: Develop Training Materials phase completion
candidates.body: Legal must stamp the frozen master spec, and HR and Ops must reply with version, timestamp, and file ID/screenshot against that spec before phase can move to Completed. Deadline: confirmation of edits needed by EOD.

candidates.subject: Training Materials Validation Loop
candidates.body: Final validation loop with Legal, HR, and business owners required before training materials can be completed.

candidates.subject: scenario content requirements
candidates.body: Each scenario must include trigger-impacting field and audit-trail impact.

candidates.subject: Manual report replacement scope freeze
candidates.body: Ownership and cadence can be locked, but 'manual-report replacements' is too broad; freeze control requirements and retirement criteria first. Spec anchored to http://sharepoint/financial_reporting_automation/define_reporting_requirements_spec ; park edge-case replacements for controlled change review.

candidates.subject: Training Materials
candidates.body: 99% complete, core content locked. Pending final validation loop with Legal, HR, and business owners.

candidates.subject: Matrix requirements
candidates.body: Matrix must include row ID, field ID, retrieval path, and approval date.

candidates.subject: Freeze approved rows
candidates.body: Approved rows to be frozen once the matrix lands.

candidates.subject: minimum fields confirmation
candidates.body: Request for @legal to confirm minimum fields: owner, approver, effective date, rationale, impacted field IDs, and citation.

recall_context.query: What is the team’s current approach for reviewing channel inconsistencies in the customer journey?

extraction_items.text: Possible spec/content mismatch between in-app flows, lifecycle emails, and support scripts. Audit against spec to isolate break.

Risk is kept open until UX locks the core flow and Support signs off on web, email, in-app, and assisted against spec http://sharepoint/customer_onboarding_optimization/risk:_inconsistent_channel_experience_spec.
Validation pass limited to mobile friction points affecting ownership, activation timing, or first-week perception. If mobile issues do not move behavior, they remain in risk lane for phase 2.
Non-mobile validation frozen on July 6, 2025.
Document containing gaps requiring same-day check by support, engineering, and analytics.
Pull support, engineering, and analytics for same-day check on gaps in Customer Journey Mapping Specification.
Spec at http://sharepoint/customer_onboarding_optimization/risk:_inconsistent_channel_experience_spec
Prioritize top-volume web, mobile, and support-assisted entry points first
Capture owner changed mid-flow as yes/no flag
Lock web, email, and in-app first; exclude assisted flow from first-pass gap list
Support's check should be a tight mismatch hunt, not a redesign loop
If web, mobile, and assisted cues reconcile, close risk as Detected
Push back on one required artifact per channel; require source-of-truth path with explicit owner and expiry for exceptions
Channel experience diverges across web, email, in-app guidance, and support; needs leadership attention to confirm ownership and approve alignment
Risk status kept as Detected until unified flow locked and Support verifies all four paths against spec.
UX assigned as owner for final journey lock.
Support to validate web, email, in-app, and assisted against spec by EOD.
Target date for alignment is 2025-07-29.
Alignment reached: standard elements must be consistent across channels; channel-specific flexibility allowed for true exceptions.
Freeze line set for July 6, 2025, contingent on Product, Support, and Ops posting EOD tags in the spec.
Blocked by requirement for Product, Support, and Ops to post EOD behavior tags in the customer journey mapping spec.

recall_context.query: What approach is the team using for regulatory requirements assessment?

extraction_items.text: Conflicting jurisdiction requirements unresolved; leadership needs to weigh precedence. 59% through risk phase; late counsel updates and possible enforcement guidance change could force rewrite of controls, reporting, implementation assumptions before 2025-07-16.
Risk review is 59% complete. Conflicting jurisdiction items consolidated into one view for prioritization.
Late jurisdiction update shifting interpretation on a requirement previously thought settled, main source of rework risk for Legal, Operations, and Reporting.
Ops stays read-only on blanks in this artifact.
Current requirements captured at http://sharepoint/regulatory_compliance_program/identify_applicable_regulations_spec
Phase is underway at 3% progress.
Requirements assessment completed.
Assessment is 95% complete; remaining work is tightening final risk readout.
Late guidance change pushed two controls higher on priority list.
Legal and Operations owners must confirm remediation approach against spec before phase can close and downstream planning proceed.
We have officially kicked off the Pilot Onboarding Launch work. Currently at 0% complete. Need cross-functional help from Support, Operations, and Engineering.
Target completion date: July 7. Protect this date to avoid churn.
Pilot Onboarding Launch Spec available at the provided URL.
Freeze core register now, park deltas for Legal and Ops against http://sharepoint/regulatory_compliance_program/identify_applicable_regulations_spec
82% complete, remaining gap is regulatory scope alignment.
SharePoint spec at http://sharepoint/customer_onboarding_optimization/risk:_regulatory_scope_ambiguity_spec
Leadership decision needed on whether to pause closeout and re-baseline risk ratings against compliance spec due to pending policy update and control coverage changes.
A final control exception has surfaced blocking phase completion. Need Legal, Security, Operations to confirm ownership and approve remediation today.
Specification for remediation approach: http://sharepoint/regulatory_compliance_program/compliance_risk_assessment_spec
Assessment at 16% complete.
Design phase for Financial Reporting Automation kicked off.

Awaiting confirmation that latest finance change request is reflected in report template spec. Need alignment from Data Engineering and QA before proceeding further. Spec: http://sharepoint/financial_reporting_automation/design_automated_report_templates_spec
Core template structure is in place.

Need team consensus on control approach by EOD: lock baseline formatting/validation rules now, or keep flexible rules layer for late regulatory updates. Preference for flexible for auditability and change traceability. Weigh-in needed from Finance Ops and Data Engineering.
Spec used for designing report templates.
Work items wrapped and baseline established. Final pass underway.
Written Ops sign-off required on two provisional control rows before completing assessment.
After pack published, @User_1 to freeze accepted set.
Pack to be published at 17:00 UTC on 2025-07-27.
Spec frozen at http://sharepoint/customer_onboarding_optimization/compliance_control_design_spec.
New requirements must be submitted by end of day 2025-07-16 to be considered.
Legal handles true ambiguity only; Ops handles evidence capture/remediation.

Closure of risk as 'Detected' is blocked pending Legal stamping owner, region, and disposition in http://sharepoint/customer_onboarding_optimization/risk:_regulatory_scope_ambiguity_spec by 2025-07-16. July 18 exceptions are docs-only.

Freeze ratings only for interpretation-dependent controls and those missing owner acceptance or evidence source. Provisional on pre-7/19 wording. All other controls continue moving against spec. 24-hour Legal cutoff applied.
24-hour Legal cutoff for interpretation-dependent controls to avoid drift into Audit week.
Owner review due same day for controls with missing source-of-truth.
Support and Engineering alignment is the main gating item; pilot stability takes priority.
Lock the 'must-stabilize' list for July 7 to separate pilot blockers from follow-up items.
Finance must confirm which audit-trace fields can alter reported content to avoid gatekeeping harmless history updates. Reference: Cobalt Close capture-only metadata stayed fast.
Legal should send the missing-jurisdiction list today, with owner and controlling authority attached.
Blank rows stay unresolved-holding; clean rows keep moving; Ops stays read-only on blanks at http://sharepoint/regulatory_compliance_program/risk:_conflicting_jurisdiction_requirements_spec.
If Legal cannot populate missing-jurisdiction list today, escalate those jurisdictions immediately and freeze any dependent mappings.
